relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Knowledge can inflate, but love builds the body.
Thinking you know may show you do not yet know rightly.
Loving the Divine means being known by the Divine.
The idol is nothing, and the Divine is one.
Many powers may be called gods and lords.
For us all things are from the one Divine and through the one Lord.
Not everyone experiences this knowledge without conscience-wounding.
Food does not increase or diminish our standing before the Divine.
Liberty must not become a stumbling block.
Knowledge displayed without love can train a tender conscience into harm.
A sibling for whom Messiah died can be damaged by your knowledge.
Wounding the sibling’s conscience wounds Messiah.
Love can abstain forever rather than make a sibling stumble.

contemplative_note: 1 Corinthians 8 makes love the test of knowledge. Correctness that wounds the body is not yet wisdom.
